headline: California SBDC Names Verizon Corporate Small Business Champion of the Year
summary: The California Small Business Development Center (SBDC) Network has named Verizon its Corporate Small Business Champion of the Year, recognizing the company's significant efforts to strengthen small businesses and expand economic opportunity across California and the nation.

headline: Verizon Announces Preliminary Shareholder Voting Results from 2026 Annual Meeting
summary: NEW YORK, May 21, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Verizon Communications Inc. (NYSE, Nasdaq: VZ) today announced the preliminary voting results from its 2026 annual meeting of shareholders. The meeting was conducted today in a virtual-only format. Verizon’s shareholders elected each of Verizon’s nine director nominees to a one-year term. headline: REX Shares Launches T-REX 2X ASTS (ASUP) & 2X LITE (LITU) ETFs
summary: MIAMI, May 21, 2026--REX Shares ("REX") and Tuttle Capital Management ("TCM") today announce the launch of the T-REX 2X Long ASTS Daily Target ETF (Cboe: ASUP) and the T-REX 2X Long LITE Daily Target ETF (Cboe: LITU), leveraged ETFs providing 2x daily long exposure to AST SpaceMobile, Inc. (Nasdaq: ASTS) and Lumentum Holdings Inc. (Nasdaq: LITE), respectively.

headline: Why retirees are banking on SCHD’s 15-year unbroken dividend streak

headline: Verizon Spectrum Approval And Cyber Alliance Add New Angles To Valuation
summary: FCC approves Verizon Communications' purchase of spectrum from Array Digital Infrastructure, clearing a path for expanded network capacity and coverage. Verizon joins other major communications companies to create the Communications Cybersecurity Information Sharing and Analysis Center, or C2 ISAC. C2 ISAC is set up as a non profit focused on sector wide cybersecurity information sharing and resilience. For investors tracking Verizon Communications (NYSE:VZ), these developments arrive with...

headline: A $2.2 Million Dividend Portfolio That Quietly Pays $13,200 a Month Without a Single MLP Tax Headache
summary: A 67-year-old couple filing jointly with $2.2 million in investable assets wants to generate $13,200 per month, or $158,400 annually, in portfolio income without dealing with the tax complexity of Schedule K-1 forms. That preference is more common than many income investors admit. Most retirees are not just seeking yield. They want dependable cash flow, ...
